# Analysis {#Anal1}

```{r tidyr5, echo = FALSE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE}

library(knitr)
opts_chunk$set(tidy.opts=list(width.cutoff=50), tidy = FALSE)

```

Now that the data have been cleaned we are ready for the trend analysis. The analysis can be done at multiple spatail scale. Here we will focus using `ProtocolCode` first. 

```{r anal.dat, echo = TRUE, message = FALSE}

#ObservationCount = Total
#ObservationCount2 = 1st Minute
#ObservationCount3 = 2nd Minute

#select desired data columns for analysis
anal.dat<-dat %>% select("CommonName", "species_id", "RouteIdentifier", "SiteCode", "SurveyAreaIdentifier","collection", "ProtocolCode", "survey_day", "survey_month", "survey_year", "CollectorNumber", "latitude", "longitude", "StateProvince", "ObservationCount", "ObservationCount2", "ObservationCount3", "date", "doy")

#Need to have seperate columns for Route and Stops. This may vary by province and may not be needed in all instanaces.   
#anal.dat<-anal.dat %>% separate(SiteCode, c("Route", "Stop"), sep="-") 
#anal.dat$Route<-as.factor(anal.dat$Route)
#anal.dat$Stop<-as.factor(anal.dat$Stop)

## Generate species list for analysis. The speices that make the cut will vary on province based on the data manipulation
species.list <- unique(anal.dat$CommonName)

```

We are ready to develope the species-specific analysis loop

```{r sp.loop, echo = TRUE, message = FALSE}

for(m in 1:length(species.list)) {

m<-1 #for testing
   
  sp.data <-NULL 
  sp.data <- filter(anal.dat, CommonName == species.list[m]) %>%
      droplevels()
  
  print(paste("Currently analyzing species ", m, "/", species.list[m], sep = "")) 

#zero fill by merging with the events dataframe. 
sp.data <- left_join(event.data, sp.data, by = c("SurveyAreaIdentifier", "RouteIdentifier", "survey_year", "survey_month", "survey_day")) %>%
             mutate(ObservationCount = replace(ObservationCount, is.na(ObservationCount), 0))
    
#count the number of owls per route as the response varaible. This may need to be changed if the number of stops on a route has changes over time. Will need to discuss with provinical leads. 
  
  sp.data<-sp.data %>% group_by(RouteIdentifier, survey_year) %>% 
    summarise(count=sum(ObservationCount)) 
 
#create the offset variable which is the number of routes run in a year to control for changes in effor over time. Previously I was using the log transformed nroute as an offset (E), however, this assumes that doubling the number of routes will double the number of owls counted, which may not hold true. The other option is to add nroute as a covariate in the model (Zuur 2018 pg 445). This was the option that was selected. It also make back tranformation easier.  
  
  nroute<-sp.data%>% group_by(survey_year) %>% summarise(nroute=n_distinct(RouteIdentifier))
  sp.data<-left_join(nroute, sp.data, by="survey_year")

#center date variables      
	sp.data <- sp.data %>% mutate(mean.yr = sum(min(survey_year) + max(survey_year))/2,
	         cyear = survey_year - mean.yr,
	         yearfac = as.factor(cyear),
	         fyear = as.factor(survey_year)) %>% 
	  select(-mean.yr)

mean.yr<- sum(min(sp.data$survey_year) + max(sp.data$survey_year))/2
		
#prepare variable for analysis
sp.data$RouteIdentifier<-as.factor(sp.data$RouteIdentifier)
sp.data<-as.data.frame(sp.data)		

###Creating the GLM model###

##Model formula for overdispersed poisson
#This is used to model overdispersion f(id.iid, model="iid")   
	
#inla.formula <- count ~ cyear + nroute 
#  f(RouteIdentifier, model = "iid") + f(yearfac, model = "ar1")+ f(id.iid, model="iid")

#Overdispersed Poisson with no spatail autocorrelation effect
# trend<-NULL
# trend<-inla(inla.formula, family = "poisson", data = data.frame(sp.data, id.iid = 1:nrow(sp.data)), control.predictor = list(compute=TRUE), control.compute = list(config=TRUE, dic=TRUE))

#Zero inflated model allows us to focus on two questions: what is driving the absence verus presence of owls, and once present, what is driving abundance. 

inla.formula <- count ~ cyear + nroute +   
  f(RouteIdentifier, model = "iid") + f(yearfac, model = "ar1")

#We might want to use a penalized complexity prioir for route (volumn I)
#hyper.idd<-list(prec=list(prioit='pc.prec', param=c(2, 0.05)))

#Create model matrix for linear combinations
myData<-NULL
myData<-data.frame(sp.data$cyear)
myData<-rename(myData, c("sp.data.cyear"="cyear"))
xmat<-model.matrix(~cyear, data=sp.data)
xmat<-as.data.frame(xmat)
lcb<-inla.make.lincombs(xmat)

trend_lcb<-inla(inla.formula, family = "zeroinflatedpoisson1", lincomb=lcb, control.inla = list(lincomb.derived.only=FALSE), control.predictor = list(compute=TRUE, quantiles=c(0.025, 0.975)), data=sp.data, control.compute = list(config=TRUE, dic=TRUE))

myData<-cbind(myData, trend_lcb$summary.lincomb[,c("mean", "0.025quant", "0.975quant")])

#Back standardize year
myData$year<-  myData$cyear+mean.yr

#Back tranform the mean count value using the helper function since we are using a log link (Zuur 2017, pg. 179). This takes alot of time. Go have a coffee. 
pred.marg<-trend_lcb$marginals.lincomb.derived
myData$mu<-unlist(lapply(pred.marg, function(x) inla.emarginal(exp,x)))
myData$selo<-unlist(lapply(pred.marg, function(x) inla.qmarginal(c(0.025), inla.tmarginal(exp,x))))
myData$seup<-unlist(lapply(pred.marg, function(x) inla.qmarginal(c(0.975), inla.tmarginal(exp,x))))
myData<-distinct(myData)

#calucalte raw route mean for plotting 
mean<-sp.data %>% group_by(survey_year) %>% summarize(mean=mean(count))
sp.data<-left_join(sp.data, mean, by="survey_year")

ggplot()+
  geom_point(data=sp.data, aes(y=mean.x, x=survey_year))+
  xlab("Year")+
  ylab("Mean Route Count")+
  ggtitle(paste("Population trends for ", species.list[m], sep=""))+
  theme_light()+
  theme(text=element_text(size=15))+
  geom_line(data=myData, aes(x=year, y=mu), colour="black")+
  geom_ribbon(data=myData, aes(x=year, ymax=seup, ymin=selo), alpha=0.3)

#Dispersion statistic 
pi.inla<-trend$summary.hyper[1,"mean"]  
mu<-trend$summary.fitted.values[,"mean"]
p<-length(trend$names.fixed)+1
ExpY<-(1-pi.inla)*mu
VarY<-(1-pi.inla *(mu+pi.inla*mu^2))
E<-(sp.data$count-ExpY)/sqrt(VarY)
N<-nrow(sp.data)
Dispersion<-sum(E^2)/(N-p)
print(paste("Dispersions Statistic = ", Dispersion, sep = "")) 
##It is likely that the model is still overdispersed. We will try to fit a GAM before deciding if a spatial component is needed.

###Creating the GAM model###

#Non-linear patterns in residual can be deal with using a quadratic of cubic term, however, they quickly result in poor model fit (Zuur 2018, pg 450). So we will try to fit a more complex model with a smoother to quantify the term 'function(cyear)'. 

#The BBbayes uses a 3rd (cubic) degree polynomial spline using the jagam function in the R-package mgcv. Similarly, we will include one knot for  every 4 years in the time-series (Smith and Edwards 2020)

#k<-trunc((max.yr-min.yr)/4)
#Need to keep constant or the code below won't work. For a 20 year time period k=4, if a 10 year time period k=2.

#We use the smoothCon function in mgcv to obtain the basis function. We do not include an intercept in the smoother 
sm<-smoothCon(s(cyear, bs="cr", k=4, fx=TRUE), data=sp.data, absorb.cons=TRUE)[[1]]
Xcr<-sm$X
nc<-ncol(Xcr)
xnames<-paste("x", 1:nc, sep="")
colnames(Xcr)<-xnames

lcs<-inla.make.lincombs(x1=Xcr[,"x1"], 
                        x2=Xcr[,"x2"], 
                        x3=Xcr[,"x3"])
sp.data2<-cbind(sp.data, Xcr)

inla.formula2<-formula(count ~ 1 + x1 + x2 + x3 + nroute +   
  f(RouteIdentifier, model = "iid") + f(yearfac, model = "ar1"))

trend_cr<-inla(inla.formula2, family = "zeroinflatedpoisson1", lincomb=lcs, control.inla = list(lincomb.derived.only=FALSE),  data=sp.data2, control.compute = list(config=TRUE, dic=TRUE), quantiles = c(0.025, 0.975))

Smoother<-trend_cr$summary.lincomb.derived
nr<-nrow(Xcr)
f<-Smoother[1:nr, "mean"]
selo<-Smoother[1:nr, "0.025quant"]
seup<-Smoother[1:nr, "0.975quant"]

myData.cr<-data.frame(mu=f, 
                   selo=selo, 
                   seup=seup, 
                   cyear=sp.data2$cyear, 
                   ID=rep("4 knots", nr))

#Back standardize year
myData.cr$year<-  myData.cr$cyear+mean.yr

ggplot()+
  geom_line(data=myData.cr, aes(x=year, y=mu))+
  geom_ribbon(data=myData.cr, aes(x=year, ymax=seup, ymin=selo), alpha=0.6)+
  xlab("Year")+
  ylab("Mean Route Count")+
  ggtitle(paste("Population trend for ", species.list[m], sep=""))+
  theme_light()

#Calculate residuals
#Res<-sp.data$count-mu
#plot(sp.data$cyear, Res)

} #end species loop


```
